LAW & MOTION/DISCOVERY, DEPT. 301, AS TO APRIL-15-2025 HEARING RE: PLAINTIFF SPENCER JACKSON'S EX PARTE APPLICATION FOR RIGHT TO ATTACH ORDER AND ORDER FOR ISSUANCE OF WRIT OF ATTACHMENT; THE COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING. ATTACHMENT IS PURELY A STATUTORY REMEDY AND A COURT MAY NOT ORDER AN ATTACHMENT PROCEDURE THAT IS NOT SPECIFICALLY PROVIDED FOR BY THE ATTACHMENT LAW. (SEE JORDAN-LYON PRODUCTIONS, LTD. V. CINEPLEX ODEON CORP. (1994) 29 CAL.APP.4TH 1459, 1466.) HERE, PLAINTIFF SPENCER JACKSON SEEKS A RIGHT TO ATTACH ORDER AND A WRIT OF ATTACHMENT BASED ON HIS SHOWING OF PROBABLE VALIDITY OF A COMMERCIAL CLAIM BASED ON A CONTRACT (SEE MINI MINUTES FOR COMPLETE RULING). ORDER SUBMITTED. JUDGE: CHRISTINE VAN AKEN; CLERK: V. DA FONSECA; NOT REPORTED. (301/CVA)

calendar.matters:
CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Sep-23-2025 Off Calendar

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Aug-11-2025 Continued To Oct-22-25 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

PLAINTIFF SPENCER JACKSON'S MOTION FOR RIGHT TO ATTACH ORDER AND ORDER FOR ISSUANCE OF WRIT OF ATTACHMENT
Off Calendar Apr-15-2025 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Hearing Re: PLAINTIFF SPENCER JACKSON'S EX PARTE APPLICATION FOR RIGHT TO ATTACH ORDER AND ORDER FOR ISSUANCE OF WRIT OF ATTACHMENT.

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Attachment is purely a statutory remedy and a court may not order an attachment procedure that is not specifically provided for by the Attachment Law. (See Jordan-Lyon Productions, Ltd. v. Cineplex Odeon Corp. (1994) 29 Cal.App.4th 1459, 1466.) Here, plaintiff Spencer Jackson seeks a right to attach order and a writ of attachment based on his showing of probable validity of a commercial claim based on a contract. Defendant Getaround, Inc. contests the amount he seeks to attach and contends there are procedural defects in his application that preclude relief (see word minutes for complete ruling.)

Order submitted.

Judge: Christine Van Aken; Clerk: V. Da Fonseca; Not reported. (301/CVA)
